task: Create a morning briefing: fetch weather for Munich from Open-Meteo, get top 3 HN stories, check my email inbox for unread count, and compile all into a summary document. Email it to kiu@ikangai.com.

Process incoming invoices: parse all invoice text files in /tmp/clive/invoices/, extract amounts and vendor names, create a summary CSV, calculate total outstanding, and send a report email to kiu@ikangai.com.
Research competitor: fetch the GitHub profile of langchain-ai/langchain (stars, recent activity), check their latest blog posts via lynx, and compile a competitive analysis document.
Prepare for a meeting with Kiu: look up their contact info in the CRM, check recent email threads with them, fetch the latest deal status, and create a one-page meeting brief.
Execute a release workflow: run tests on the current codebase, generate release notes from git log, create a version tag, build a tarball, and draft a release announcement tweet (under 280 chars).

Create a data-driven report: download exchange rates from the ER API for EUR, fetch the latest BTC price from CoinGecko, process local sales CSV data, and combine into a financial dashboard document with charts (ASCII).

Set up a new team member: create their project directory structure, generate SSH key pair, configure SSH config for the remote server, create their email account config, and write an onboarding checklist document.
Respond to an incident: check if the production server ssh.ikangai.com is reachable, check disk space and memory, review recent error logs, create an incident report, and send it to kiu@ikangai.com.

Generate a weekly standup report: pull completed tasks from git log (last 7 days), check open issues in the local task tracker, check calendar for upcoming deadlines, and format as a standup document.

Compile a weekly tech newsletter: scrape HN top stories, fetch GitHub trending repos, get the latest posts from r/programming via Reddit JSON API, write editorial summaries for each, and format as HTML email.
Verify backup integrity: create local backups of key directories, compute checksums, sync to remote server via rsync, verify remote checksums match, and generate a backup report.

Create a client proposal: fetch the client's company info from their website using lynx, pull their deal data from the CRM, calculate pricing based on their requirements, and generate a formatted proposal document. Save it and email it to kiu@ikangai.com.

Run a security audit: check SSL certificates for ikangai.com and ssh.ikangai.com, scan local open ports, verify SSH key strengths, check for world-writable files in /tmp/clive/, and compile a security report.
Reconcile expenses: download bank statement (from local CSV), match against submitted expense reports, flag discrepancies, generate a reconciliation report, and email it to kiu@ikangai.com.

Monitor API health: check response times for 5 public APIs (httpbin, jsonplaceholder, github, open-meteo, restcountries), log results, flag any with response time > 2s, and send a summary email if any are slow.
